romanized: Mai La Ji Chi 4 Kuai Chao Zhi Tao Can

food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — battered fried chicken wings with french fries and soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general fried chicken class (FOODON_00002372); no specific entry exists for battered chicken wings or McDonald's McWings combo meal. Closest_parent match used for the chicken wings component; the full upsized meal (wings + fries + drink) has no direct FoodOn class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKK covers coated and fried chicken meat, which is the primary component of McWings. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for the full combo meal including fries and beverage. Closest_parent match applied to the dominant food component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept found for battered fried chicken wings fast food meal. SNOMED CT does not routinely code commercial fast food combo meals.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (4 McWings + large fries + large soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consists of 4 battered fried chicken wings (approximately 240 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 170 g), and one large soft drink (approximately 240 ml / 240 g equivalent), totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — McWings, French Fries (Large), Soft Drink (Large); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried battered chicken wings and fast food french fries
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (primary menu nutrition disclosure) / USDA Agricultural Research Service FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data for McWings, large fries, and large soft drink separately. The per-100g composite was derived by summing the three components weighted by their estimated gram contributions (McWings 240g, large fries 170g, large soft drink 240g = 650g total) and dividing by 650g. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries (NDB 05062 — Chicken, wing, meat and skin, coated, fried; NDB 21137 — Fast foods, potato, french fried) were used to cross-validate macro ratios. Where McDonald's Singapore values were available they took precedence. Soft drink component (cola, large) was estimated using USDA FoodData Central entry for cola-type carbonated beverage.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried potato (french fries) and battered fried chicken
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's McWings 4-Piece Upsized Meal as a composite. GI was estimated as a weighted average of the three components: (1) battered fried chicken wings — GI approximately 45 (low; protein and fat dominant, minimal glycemic carbohydrate contribution), weighted at 240g; (2) large french fries — GI approximately 75 (high; University of Sydney GI Database, french fries, fast food), weighted at 170g; (3) large cola soft drink — GI approximately 63 (medium; University of Sydney GI Database, cola beverage), weighted at 240g. Carbohydrate-weighted GI calculation: [(45 × 4g carb from wings) + (75 × 47g carb from fries) + (63 × 62g carb from cola)] / (4+47+62) = approximately 65. GL per serving = GI × total carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 65 × 152.75 / 100 ≈ 35.2. Classified as medium GI (56–69 range).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 2015 mg sodium per full upsized meal serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 87% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, primarily from the seasoned battered chicken wings, salted fries, and soft drink; substituting water for the soft drink and requesting unsalted fries would meaningfully reduce sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ≈ 35) and high refined carbohydrate content from french fries and cola soft drink
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence only, as the combination of high-GI french fries (GI ~75) and sugar-sweetened cola produces a glycemic load of approximately 35 per serving, which is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; replacing the cola with water and the large fries with a side salad would substantially reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and ultra-processed composition
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is not recommended for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its content of saturated fat (~13.7 g per serving), trace trans fat (~0.65 g per serving), high sodium (~2015 mg per serving), and ultra-processed ingredients including deep-fried battered chicken and commercial french fries, all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: McDonald's McWings 4-Piece Upsized Meal is a fast food combo meal sold at McDonald's restaurants in Singapore, consisting of four battered and deep-fried chicken wings, one large portion of salted french fries, and one large soft drink (typically cola). The chicken wings are coated in a light seasoned wheat flour batter and deep-fried in vegetable oil, producing a crispy exterior with juicy chicken meat inside. The meal is certified Halal and is a popular value option at McDonald's Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, the full meal is energy-dense, providing approximately 1268 kcal per serving, with significant contributions from fat (deep-frying oil and chicken skin), refined carbohydrates (fries and cola sugar), and sodium (seasoning and salt). The per-100g composite profile shows approximately 195 kcal, 7.8 g protein, 8.5 g fat, and 23.5 g carbohydrate. The glycemic load of the full meal is high (approximately 35), driven primarily by the large fries and sugar-sweetened cola. This meal is classified as ultra-processed and is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and high glycemic load. Occasional consumption in the context of an otherwise balanced diet is the recommended approach for health-conscious individuals.
common_user_queries: How many calories in McDonald's McWings upsized meal?
McDonald's McWings meal carbs and sugar
Is McWings meal suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat McWings with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data for McWings, large fries, and large soft drink on their official nutrition page, but does not publish a single composite per-100g figure for the full upsized meal. The per-100g values were derived by the Veritas engine through a weighted-average calculation across the three components using McDonald's Singapore published per-item data cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries. The GI is an analogous estimate using University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high, as no single approved-source entry covers the exact composite meal.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from McDonald's Singapore published per-item nutrition data (McWings 4pc, large fries, large soft drink) weighted by estimated gram contributions (240g + 170g + 240g = 650g total serving). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries were used for cross-validation. 2. Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reported in McDonald's Singapore nutrition disclosures. Similarly, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all -1 because they are not reported in the primary source (McDonald's Singapore nutrition disclosures) and precise composite calculation from secondary sources was not feasible. 3. GI of 65 (medium) is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite meal exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Component GI values used: battered fried chicken wings ~45 (low), fast food french fries ~75 (high), cola soft drink ~63 (medium); carbohydrate-weighted average applied. 4. Serving size of 650g is an estimate based on typical McDonald's Singapore upsized meal component weights; actual weights may vary slightly by outlet. 5. The meal is listed as Halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore. 6. Trans fat is present at trace levels (~0.1g per 100g) from deep-frying; confirmed from USDA SR Legacy cross-reference. 7. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are closest_parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this composite fast food meal.
